title: rubus × agrestis var. mixtus
intro: Rubus × agrestis var. mixtus, also known as the mixed bramble, is a hybrid plant species in the Rubus genus, resulting from the cross between Rubus fruticosus and Rubus idaeus. The medicinal properties of Rubus × agrestis var. mixtus include its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory content, which is attributed to its rich polyphenol composition. The plant has been traditionally used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps and menopausal hot flashes, showcasing its potential health benefits. Rubus × agrestis var. mixtus can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
